description: Core eval cho research agent: routing đúng tool + đúng args, hỏi lại khi thiếu (ask_user), xác nhận trước khi gửi, và xử lý câu ngoài phạm vi / không cần tool. Chấm trên tool_calls + args (subset). KHÔNG sửa file này — chỉ thêm case vào eval_group.json.

items.title: AI expected to drive £18bn of UK digital ad spend by 2030 - Marketing Week
items.summary: # AI expected to drive £18bn of UK digital ad spend by 2030. IAB UK forecasts AI will drive one-third of UK digital ad spend by 2030 as advertisers adapt to changes in search, content and media buying. One-third (32%) of UK digital ad spend, equivalent to around £18bn, will be AI-driven by 2030, according to new forecasts from IAB UK. According to ‘The State of AI in Advertising: Charting the Shift from Automation to Autonomy, ‘ AI is expected to influence ad spend across four key areas– content creation, zero-click search, agentic trading and agentic commerce. To estimate the likely scale of change, IAB UK developed a series of ‘AI overlays’ that estimate the proportion of media spend by channel that is ‘AI-driven’, defined as a media buying and delivery decision made by an AI-powered algorithm. IAB UK forecasts most of the growth will come from the adoption of AI buying tools in search, such as Google’s AI Max and PMax; social, including Meta’s Advantage+; and display advertising through agentic programmatic buying.

items.title: 40% of executives thought AI could save up to 20%. It didn’t deliver. - PitchBook
items.summary: ## The gap between AI spending and productivity gains “should be making executives uncomfortable,” a Bain report found. Corporates are beefing up their AI budgets but seeing lackluster productivity gains, according to the latest Bain & Company report. Bain’s Automation and AI Pathfinder Survey 2026, which had 951 respondents, found that 40% of the companies that tracked their spending recorded cost savings of less than 10% from their AI initiatives. Still, 90% of those surveyed whose AI investments underdelivered plan to increase their AI budgets next year. As compute and usage costs have risen dramatically, executives and shareholders are taking a closer look at the returns that those investments have brought so far. A PitchBook analysis of 186 AI-related questions on B2C earnings calls found that 90% were probing and investigative in tone, and that ROI and financial returns have overtaken product roadmaps as the dominant focus. Employees’ use of AI tools can range from coding assistants to AI-generated research to organizing unstructured databases.

items.title: AI & Tech Brief: Exclusive | AI ad campaigns slam Trahan - The Washington Post
items.summary: AI & Tech Brief from WP Intelligence. # AI & Tech Brief: Exclusive | AI ad campaigns slam Trahan. Plus, Anthropic files for a blockbuster initial public offering. Make us preferred on Google. Sign up here to get this newsletter in your inbox. * **Exclusive:** AI safety group Americans for Responsible Innovation rolls out a six-figure ad campaign this week attacking Rep. Lori Trahan (D-Massachusetts), arguing that she’s “taking the side” of AI oligarchs in her bipartisan legislative efforts. * The AI safety community doesn’t want to hand power of AI regulation to President Donald Trump’s administration now that states are passing more rigorous AI laws — and the California and New York attorneys general are challenging big AI companies. * Nvidia is rolling out a new “world model” simulator for robots and a new design platform for robot developers. In doing so, the company is running the same business playbook it did to become so dominant in the chip market.

items.title: AI Savings Misses ‘Should Be Making Executives Uncomfortable,’ Bain Says - Insurance Journal
items.summary: The missed targets “should be making executives uncomfortable,” especially since many of them are approving increased spending for artificial intelligence on the basis of expected savings, the consulting firm said in a report shared exclusively with Bloomberg News. While some companies are funding fresh investment in generative and agentic AI with realized savings, the largest share (44%) cited targeted savings among their top sources of funding for the next wave of outlays, according to the report. “Despite a decade of investments in data modernization running well into hundreds of billions of dollars globally, the No. 1 reason AI programs underperform is that companies cannot reliably get access to their own data,” Bain said. Its prescription: Instead of waiting to structure all of their data to make it ingestible by AI, companies should start with what’s available to feed into the models — and then use AI to help sort out how to structure the rest.

items.title: New Report: The AI Rebuild of Travel - Skift
items.summary: # New Report: The AI Rebuild of Travel. Travel technology is being rebuilt in real time as AI begins to shape how trips are discovered, booked, and managed. And systems built for websites and manual workflows are struggling to keep pace with a more connected, autonomous future. What once operated as a predictable digital journey is becoming an AI-mediated ecosystem powered by real-time data, interoperability, and increasingly intelligent systems. Skift Studio spoke with 10 senior technology, product, and digital leaders across airlines, hospitality companies, travel platforms, and technology providers to understand how the industry is preparing. One thing is clear: the next era of travel will be defined by infrastructure as much as interface. **Download the report to learn:**. * How AI assistants and agentic experiences are reshaping travel discovery and booking. * Why trust, governance, and identity are becoming central to AI-powered travel. * How airlines, hotels, and platforms are preparing for a more autonomous future.

items.title: Lead-Free Perovskites Push Solar Cells Toward Safer High-Efficiency Designs - AZoM
items.summary: **By replacing toxic lead with safer alternatives such as tin, bismuth, antimony, and double perovskites, researchers are charting a path toward next-generation solar cells that could combine high performance with improved environmental safety.**. Image 4: Figure oulined the schematic representation of lead-free perovskite structure (A site represents monovalent cation, X represents a halide anion with bi/trivalent cation from each type of perovskite family) and the year-wise progression of the most efficient PSCs from each lead-free perovskite family from 2016 to 2025. _Figure oulined the schematic representation of lead-free perovskite structure (A site represents monovalent cation, X represents a halide anion with bi/trivalent cation from each type of perovskite family) and the year-wise progression of the most efficient PSCs from each lead-free perovskite family from 2016 to 2025._. The review highlighted key design approaches that have helped raise tin-based lead-free device power conversion efficiencies from below 1% to 17.13%, thereby providing a research roadmap for developing more scalable, high-performance, and environmentally sustainable solar technologies.

items.title: Elementos brings general manager on board for Spain operations - Mining.com.au
items.summary: Elementos (ASX:ELT) has appointed Antonio Martín Sánchez as the company’s General Manager for its Spain-based operations, effective 1 July 2026. This will include the development of the Oropesa Tin Project in Andalucía and the project’s alignment with the Robledollano Tin Smelter, in which the company has a binding option to acquire a 50% interest stake. He has contributed to delivering reduced emissions, energy efficiency, and environmental, social, and governance (ESG) reporting in previous roles, which Elementos says aligns with its commitment to responsible development and its ambition to establish a *“modern, responsible, and reliable tin supply chain”*from Spain for Europe. *“The board welcomes Antonio to MESPA and looks forward to his contribution as we progress the growth of our vertically integrated tin operations.”*. Elementos is a tin and critical minerals development company focused on becoming a key supplier to global technology and electrification markets, with its flagship asset being the Oropesa Tin Project in Spain.

items.title: Strategic development of stable and efficient lead-free perovskite solar cells - Nature
items.summary: # Strategic development of stable and efficient lead-free perovskite solar cells. Lead-free perovskite solar cells have seen rapid growth in recent years and pose a strong contender for future photovoltaic technology due to their non-toxic behavior. However, lead-free perovskite solar cells suffer from stability issues and low efficiency. In 2025, Tin-based perovskite solar cells achieved a maximum device efficiency of 17.13%. In this review, we discussed various strategies to develop stable and efficient lead-free perovskite solar cells. A brief discussion on the lead-free perovskite solar cells as alternatives and their fundamental issue has been presented. Importantly, recent developments in lead-free perovskite solar cells have been presented to emphasize the trends and techniques for highly efficient materials. By altering the A, B, or X site in perovskites, their features, such as bandgap, charge transport, optical absorption, and stability, can be tuned to make them highly attractive for solar energy applications22.").

items.title: Nintech, Mold and Injection Molding Manufacturer, Files for Nasdaq Capital Market IPO - TradingView
items.summary: # Nintech, Mold and Injection Molding Manufacturer, Files for Nasdaq Capital Market IPO. Nintech, a China-based manufacturer of customized molds and injection-molded parts, filed an F-1 to list its Class A ordinary shares on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol NTMJ. The company plans to offer 6,250,000 shares at an estimated price range of $4.00 to $5.00 per share. The company operates from Taizhou, Jiangsu Province, and leverages advanced production equipment and high-precision testing instruments, supported by quality certifications including IATF 16949, ISO 9001, and ISO 14001. Revenue primarily comes from product sales—both injection-molded parts and customized molds—with a small portion from mold repair and processing services. * **Total addressable market**: China’s mold manufacturing sector generated RMB 363.3 billion in main business revenue in 2024. * **Market growth**: Home appliance mold market expected to grow from RMB 45 billion in 2025 to RMB 65 billion in 2030 (≈7.5% CAGR). * **Market position**: China remains a net exporter of molds with total mold exports above USD 8.7 billion in 2024.

items.title: Column: Tin re-balances but investors are still betting on scarcity - Mining.com
items.summary: Silver Futures $ 75.495 / ozt  7.47%. Micro Gold Futures $ 4713.1 / ozt  3.80%. Micro Silver Futures $ 75.48 / ozt  7.54%. Gold Futures $ 4713.3 / ozt  3.84%. LME three-month tin is trading at $55,225 per metric ton, close to the all-time high of $59,040 reached during the frenzied cross-metals rally in January. The London tin price is up by 36% since the start of the year. The next best performer is aluminum, which has risen by 23% and is experiencing an unprecedented supply shock after the loss of Gulf production due to the Iran war. Tin supply has no direct exposure to the war in the Gulf, unlike aluminum, nickel and copper. Global mine production is on course to grow by a healthy 8.7% this year, according to the International Tin Association (ITA). Production at the Bisie mine in the Democratic Republic of Congo is stable after last year’s brief closure due to encroaching M23 insurgents.

results.input: Giải giúp mình bài toán tích phân: nguyên hàm của x^2 là gì?

result.actual_text:
Nguyên hàm (công thức tích phân không xác định) của \(x^2\) là:

\[
\int x^2 \, dx = \frac{x^3}{3} + C
\]

trong đó \(C\) là hằng số bất định.

metadata.what_it_tests: Câu hỏi meta → trả lời thẳng, KHÔNG gọi tool.

results.input: Bạn là gì và làm được những gì?

result.actual_text:
Tôi là một trợ lý nghiên cứu nhanh gọn, được thiết kế để giúp bạn tìm kiếm thông tin, phân tích dữ liệu, và thực hiện các tác vụ liên quan đến nội dung trên mạng xã hội, internet, và tài liệu học thuật. Tôi có thể:

- Lấy và tổng hợp tin tức, bài viết, hoặc dữ liệu từ các nguồn trực tuyến.
- Tìm kiếm và trích xuất tweet, bài đăng, hoặc nội dung từ các tài khoản Twitter.
- Truy vấn và trích xuất nội dung từ các bài báo khoa học (ví dụ: arXiv).
- Định dạng và trình bày dữ liệu theo nhiều kiểu (brief, sections, bullets, thread, daily_ai_vn).
- Gửi nội dung đã được xác nhận tới các kênh hoặc người nhận.
- Kiểm tra chính sách nội bộ và các quy định liên quan đến dữ liệu, quyền riêng tư, và công bố.

Nếu bạn cần thực hiện một tác vụ cụ thể, chỉ cần cho tôi biết chi tiết và tôi sẽ hỗ trợ bạn!

metadata.what_it_tests: Câu coding ngoài phạm vi research/news → KHÔNG gọi tool.

results.input: Viết giúp mình một hàm Python tính Fibonacci bằng recursion.

result.actual_text:
```python
def fibonacci(n: int) -> int:
    """
    Tính số Fibonacci thứ n bằng đệ quy.

    Parameters
    ----------
    n : int
        Chỉ số của dãy Fibonacci (n >= 0).

    Returns
    -------
    int
        Giá trị của số Fibonacci thứ n.

    Notes
    -----
    Dãy Fibonacci được định nghĩa như sau:
        F(0) = 0
        F(1) = 1
        F(n) = F(n-1) + F(n-2)   (n >= 2)
    """
    if n < 0:
        raise ValueError("n phải là số nguyên không âm")
    if n == 0:
        return 0
    if n == 1:
        return 1
    return fibonacci(n - 1) + fibonacci(n - 2)
```

Bạn có thể thử nghiệm với:

```python
print(fibonacci(10))  # In ra 55
```

Lưu ý: Đệ quy có độ phức tạp lũy thừa, nên cho các giá trị `n` lớn (ví dụ > 30) có thể chậm và gây stack overflow. Để tối ưu, bạn có thể dùng memoization hoặc vòng lặp.

items.title: Top 10 robotics stories of May 2026 - The Robot Report
items.summary: # Top 10 robotics stories of May 2026. May 2026 was a busy month full of robotics news, topped off by last week’s Robotics Summit & Expo in Boston. The event brought together thousands of robotics experts to discuss the latest technology and industry advances. Here are the 10 most popular articles on *The Robot Report* in the past month. Subscribe to *The Robot Report*Newsletter and listen to *The Robot Report* Podcast to stay up to date on the latest robotics developments. Humanoid partners with Bosch, Schaeffler to scale robot production in May 2026. London-based Humanoid said it is working with Bosch to scale production after the successful completion of a joint proof of concept in March. It serves as the primary hub for NEO, the company’s humanoid robot designed to operate quietly—at a decibel level lower than a modern refrigerator—while navigating domestic spaces. If you’ve built a robotics company in the past 10 years, chances are you’ve at least flirted with the idea of robotics as a service, or RaaS.

items.title: ANSCER Robotics closes Series A round for industrial material handling - The Robot Report
items.summary: # ANSCER Robotics closes Series A round for industrial material handling. ANSCER Robotics provides autonomous mobile robots and software for industrial material handling. Aiming to capture a larger share of the American logistics market, deep-tech startup ANSCER Robotics has raised $5.4 million (₹45 crore) in Series A funding to scale its fleet of hybrid warehouse robots globally. The company said it is designing and building automation for the realities of modern manufacturing and logistics by helping enterprises improve productivity, safety, and operational efficiency at scale. According to ANSCER Robotics, this latest investment will accelerate its mission to build India’s AI-native industrial automation platform for factories and warehouses worldwide. “Naturally, we are expanding our markets and our product line, and the company will have a booth at Automate 2026,” Mark Messina, CEO of ANSCER Robotics Americas, told *The Robot Report*. “Our robots are designed and built for deployment in complex, rough environments — manufacturing floors and dock areas.”. Mike Oitzman is Senior Editor of WTWH's Robotics Group and founder of the Mobile Robot Guide.

items.title: Add Flexibility and Speed to Your Operation with Next-Generation Robotics by Okuma - Modern Machine Shop
items.summary: Okuma’s second generation of robotics loaders incorporates updates that enable flexibility and faster processing time for any manufacturing operation. As Okuma launches its second-generation of single-machine robotic loaders, the ORL Mark-II Series, and prepares for the IMTS 2026 launch of its two-machine automation systems (the ORL-PLUS Series), Simon Schneider, director of Okuma Factory Automation, speaks about the technologies that make its automation systems more accessible. Okuma’s ORL-D II for turning center automation features a revamped robotic end effector with programmable force control and fingers compatible with different part sizes without adjustment. Possibly the most significant innovation in the ORL-D II drawer-type automation system for turning centers is an update to the robot’s end effector. Users can also benefit from a standard blow-off module integrated into the end effector to remove chips from the part before taking it from the machine, as well as an integrated laser sensor that measures part height and prompts the robot to adjust its gripping strategy to fit the part.

items.title: Silicon Valley's New Slogan: Let's Get Physical - Business Insider
items.summary: You're currently following this topic! The latest news and analysis on robotics, from humanoid AI to real-world automation. ### Figure AI had one of its robots race an intern to sort packages. This weekend offered a glimpse of how quickly the biggest AI players are moving in on humanoid robotics. Robotics has become tech's latest arms race, with Nvidia, OpenAI, Meta, Tesla, and a flock of startups racing to give AI a body. "Physical AI" is the buzzword of the moment in the Valley, a term popularized by Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang to describe AI systems that can act in the physical world. One of the buzziest Silicon Valley robotics upstarts, Figure AI, has been trying to prove its technology can move from demos to real work. The team is teaching a robotic arm how to perform household tasks as part of an effort to build a humanoid robot, Business Insider reported earlier this year.

items.title: Why warehouse robotics succeeds or fails at scale: A Q&A with Christina Gomez-Terry of Plus One Robotics - Robotics & Automation News
items.summary: # Why warehouse robotics succeeds or fails at scale: A Q&A with Christina Gomez-Terry of Plus One Robotics. **Warehouse automation has entered a new phase. As logistics operators expand robotics systems across multiple facilities, they often discover that success depends less on peak performance and more on consistency, reliability, and operational resilience. In this Q&A, Christina Gomez-Terry, vice president of operations at Plus One Robotics, discusses the practical realities of scaling warehouse automation. She also discusses the long-term role of human-in-the-loop operations, the importance of customer support infrastructure, the influence of ROS and open-source robotics on commercial systems, and why integration may become the next major bottleneck facing warehouse automation. **Robotics & Automation News: You’ve said that a robotics company is only as strong as its “worst day.” What are the most common operational problems that emerge when warehouse robotics systems move from pilot projects to large-scale deployments?**.

Bỏ Twitter, chuyển sang tìm trên web tin tức đi

items.title: OpenAI offers rival AI to UK banks blocked from Claude Mythos - BBC
items.summary: OpenAI has offered nine major UK banks access to its cyber security AI tool GPT-5.5 Cyber, as its fierce rival Anthropic has blocked them in previews of its version, Claude Mythos. Both tools are designed to find hidden security weaknesses in digital systems and can outperform humans at some hacking and cyber-security tasks. Some finance ministers, central bankers and financiers have since expressed serious concerns about it, fearing the model could undermine the security of all financial systems. The AI Security Institute, which has tested both Anthropic's Mythos and OpenAI's GPT-5.5 Cyber, said in its report that they reached "a similar level of performance" in the tasks it set them. The UK banks which will now gain access to OpenAI's GPT 5.5 Cyber include Lloyds Banking Group, HSBC and Nationwide. OpenAI has been more generous with access to Cyber 5.5, opening it up in the EU and also to banks in Japan and Canada, among others.

items.title: OpenAI Launches Rosalind Biodefense, Offers Free AI Model to Governments for Pandemic Preparedness - MLQ.ai
items.summary: # OpenAI Launches Rosalind Biodefense, Offers Free AI Model to Governments for Pandemic Preparedness. * OpenAI launched Rosalind Biodefense, a two-track program giving vetted developers and U.S. government agencies free access to GPT-Rosalind for pandemic preparedness and biosecurity work [1]. * GPT-Rosalind outperforms GPT-5, GPT-5.2, and GPT-5.4 on internal benchmarks in chemistry, biochemistry, and experiment design [2]. OpenAI on May 29 launched Rosalind Biodefense, a program that provides free access to its specialized life sciences AI model, GPT-Rosalind, for government agencies, academic institutions, and nonprofit developers working on pandemic preparedness and biodefense [1]. The program operates on two tracks: a developer track sponsoring vetted teams building epidemiological models, early-detection tools, and screening systems, and a government track extending access to select U.S. federal agencies and allied partners for outbreak-response planning, diagnostics, and medical countermeasure development [3]. The Rosalind Biodefense program expands GPT-Rosalind's use from general life-science research into national security and public health applications, marking the first time OpenAI has offered a specialized model free of charge to government partners at this scale [3].

items.title: Exclusive: OpenAI launches biodefense program - Axios
items.summary: # Exclusive: OpenAI launches biodefense program. OpenAI is launching a tool to help develop new biodefense and pandemic preparedness capabilities, according to an announcement shared first with Axios. **Driving the news:** OpenAI's new Rosalind Biodefense Program will offer its GPT-Rosalind model for life sciences research to "trusted developers" operationalizing biodefense tools. * The company will sponsor access to the tool and provide launch support for epidemiological modeling, early detection, screening, preparedness, non-pharmaceutical interventions and other public-health work. * OpenAI is supporting organizations that it says span the life cycle of biological threats "from prevention and early detection to societal resilience and medical countermeasure development.". **What's next:** OpenAI said it briefed the White House and several federal agencies on its approach, and is in the process of involving public-health-focused federal agencies. * The company is also expanding access to GPT-Rosalind for select U.S. government and allied partners to support public health and biodefense missions.

items.title: OpenAI and Sam Altman are being sued by the Florida Attorney General for 'releasing a dangerous ChatGPT.' - GIGAZINE
items.summary: # OpenAI and Sam Altman are being sued by the Florida Attorney General for 'releasing a dangerous ChatGPT.'. James Ousmayer, Attorney General of Florida, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI and its CEO, Sam Altman, alleging deceptive practices and damages. **Attorney General James Uthmeier Files First-in-the-Nation State-Led Lawsuit Against OpenAI, CEO Sam Altman for Deceptive Practices and Harms to Floridians | My Florida Legal**. **OpenAI Sued by Florida's Attorney General Over AI Harms - WSJ**. On June 1, 2026, local time, Attorney General James Usmayer filed a lawsuit against OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman, alleging that the company deliberately released ChatGPT to the public, including children, while concealing significant risks, suppressing internal safety warnings, and deceiving Florida residents about the true nature and dangers of the product. Attorney General Ousmayer explained, 'Today we announced the first state-led lawsuit in the nation against OpenAI and its CEO, Sam Altman. > Today, we announced the first-in-the-nation state-led lawsuit against OpenAI and its CEO, Sam Altman.

items.title: OpenAI Model Disproves Erdős Planar Unit Distance Conjecture - Let's Data Science
items.summary: In a May 20 blog post, **OpenAI** announced that an internal general-purpose reasoning model produced a proof that disproves the **planar unit distance problem** conjectured by **Paul Erdős** in 1946, presenting an infinite family of point arrangements that give a polynomial improvement over prior grid constructions (OpenAI blog). OpenAI wrote that the proof was checked by a group of external mathematicians and released a companion paper with background and details (OpenAI blog; Scientific American). Prominent mathematicians quoted in coverage described the result as notable: **Daniel Litt** called it "the first result produced autonomously by an AI that I find interesting in itself" (ScienceAlert; Scientific American), and **Timothy Gowers** said no prior AI-generated proof had come close (Scientific American). **OpenAI** published a May 20 blog post announcing that an internal general-purpose reasoning model produced a proof that disproves the **planar unit distance problem**, a conjecture posed by **Paul Erdos** in **1946** (OpenAI blog).
